_instructions: 医学生填写。这是50篇文献的人工标注标准答案，用于评估Agent提取准确率。为每篇文献填写17个字段的标准答案。
_usage: 选择50篇PubMed文献（覆盖不同研究类型：RCT、队列研究、病例对照、基础研究等），逐篇填写gold_fields。不需要填的字段留空字符串。

gold_fields.objective: To evaluate the efficacy and safety of osimertinib as first-line treatment in patients with EGFR-mutated advanced non-small cell lung cancer.
gold_fields.method: Phase III double-blind RCT, patients with EGFR-mutated advanced NSCLC randomized 1:1 to osimertinib 80mg QD or comparator EGFR-TKIs (gefitinib/erlotinib).
gold_fields.key_findings: Median PFS 18.9 months vs 10.2 months (HR 0.46, 95% CI 0.37-0.57, p<0.001). CNS progression less frequent with osimertinib.
gold_fields.result_value: PFS HR=0.46, p<0.001; CNS progression HR=0.48
gold_fields.conclusion: Osimertinib showed significantly longer progression-free survival than standard EGFR-TKIs as first-line therapy for EGFR-mutated advanced NSCLC, with a similar safety profile.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: CONSORT diagram; Figure 2: Kaplan-Meier PFS curves; Figure 3: CNS progression analysis
gold_fields.limitations: Interim analysis; CNS imaging not mandatory per protocol; shorter follow-up for overall survival

gold_fields.objective: To evaluate pembrolizumab versus chemotherapy in PD-L1 positive advanced NSCLC patients.
gold_fields.method: Phase III double-blind RCT, 1,274 patients with advanced NSCLC and PD-L1 TPS ≥1%, randomized to pembrolizumab 200mg Q3W vs chemotherapy.
gold_fields.key_findings: Pembrolizumab significantly improved OS vs chemotherapy in PD-L1 positive NSCLC. OS HR=0.81 (95% CI 0.71-0.93, p<0.001).
gold_fields.conclusion: Pembrolizumab is superior to chemotherapy as first-line therapy for advanced NSCLC with PD-L1 expression.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: CONSORT diagram; Figure 2: Kaplan-Meier OS curves; Figure 3: Subgroup analysis
gold_fields.limitations: Open-label design; patients with EGFR/ALK mutations excluded; limited to PD-L1 positive patients

gold_fields.objective: To investigate the association between EGFR mutations and gefitinib response in lung adenocarcinoma.
gold_fields.method: Retrospective analysis of 1,200 lung adenocarcinoma patients with EGFR genotyping and clinical outcome data.
gold_fields.biomarker: EGFR mutation (exon 19 deletion, L858R, or other)
gold_fields.key_findings: EGFR mutations detected in 45% of patients. Mutant patients showed ORR 71.2% vs 1.1% in wild-type (OR=3.2, p<0.001).
gold_fields.conclusion: EGFR mutation is a strong predictor of gefitinib response in lung adenocarcinoma.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: Mutation spectrum of EGFR; Figure 2: Waterfall plot of tumor shrinkage
gold_fields.limitations: Retrospective design; single institution; lack of central pathology review

gold_fields.objective: To determine the association between APOE4 genotype and Alzheimer's disease risk through meta-analysis.
gold_fields.method: Meta-analysis of 45 case-control studies, 45,000 individuals, APOE genotyping.
gold_fields.key_findings: APOE4 carriers have 3-fold increased risk of late-onset Alzheimer's disease. OR=3.0, 95% CI 2.5-3.6, p<0.001.
gold_fields.conclusion: APOE4 is the strongest genetic risk factor for late-onset Alzheimer's disease.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: Forest plot of 45 studies; Figure 2: Funnel plot for publication bias
gold_fields.limitations: Publication bias; heterogeneity across studies; case-control design limits causal inference

gold_fields.objective: To evaluate CD19 CAR-T cell therapy in pediatric relapsed B-cell ALL.
gold_fields.method: Phase II single-arm trial, 75 pediatric patients with relapsed B-cell ALL, CD19 CAR-T cell infusion.
gold_fields.key_findings: Complete remission rate 81% with manageable cytokine release syndrome. CRS grade≥3 in 15%.
gold_fields.conclusion: CD19 CAR-T therapy is highly effective in pediatric relapsed B-cell ALL with manageable toxicity.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: Consort diagram; Figure 2: CRS grading by cytokine levels
gold_fields.limitations: Single-arm; no control group; long-term follow-up ongoing; small sample size

gold_fields.objective: To evaluate PARP inhibitor maintenance therapy in BRCA-mutated ovarian cancer.
gold_fields.method: Phase III RCT, 400 patients with BRCA-mutated ovarian cancer, PARP inhibitor vs placebo maintenance after chemotherapy.
gold_fields.key_findings: PARP inhibitor maintenance improved PFS in BRCA-mutated ovarian cancer. HR=0.30, 95% CI 0.19-0.48, p<0.001.
gold_fields.conclusion: PARP inhibitor maintenance therapy is standard of care for BRCA-mutated ovarian cancer.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: PFS Kaplan-Meier curve; Figure 2: Subgroup analysis forest plot
gold_fields.limitations: Limited to BRCA-mutated patients, no OS data yet, long-term safety unknown

gold_fields.objective: To assess the prognostic significance of KRAS mutations in pancreatic cancer.
gold_fields.method: Retrospective analysis of 1,100 pancreatic cancer patients with KRAS genotyping.
gold_fields.key_findings: KRAS mutations present in 89% of patients, associated with poor OS. HR=1.8, 95% CI 1.4-2.3, p<0.001.
gold_fields.conclusion: KRAS mutation is a negative prognostic factor in pancreatic cancer.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: Mutation prevalence; Figure 2: OS Kaplan-Meier curve
gold_fields.limitations: Retrospective, heterogeneous treatment regimens, single-center

gold_fields.objective: To identify acquired resistance mechanisms to osimertinib in NSCLC.
gold_fields.method: NGS analysis of 150 NSCLC patients with acquired resistance to osimertinib.
gold_fields.key_findings: Acquired resistance mechanisms: MET amplification (15%), C797S mutation (20%), EGFR amplification (12%).
gold_fields.conclusion: Osimertinib resistance is heterogeneous with multiple genomic mechanisms.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: Resistance mechanism pie chart; Figure 2: On-target vs off-target resistance
gold_fields.limitations: Small sample size, ctDNA vs tissue biopsy heterogeneity, lack of functional validation

gold_fields.objective: To investigate MYC expression and its role in hepatocellular carcinoma progression.
gold_fields.method: Immunohistochemistry analysis of 262 HCC samples, correlation with clinical outcomes.
gold_fields.key_findings: MYC overexpression in 62% of HCC samples, associated with aggressive tumor behavior and poor prognosis. HR=2.1, p<0.01.
gold_fields.conclusion: MYC is a negative prognostic marker in hepatocellular carcinoma.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: IHC staining representative images; Figure 2: OS Kaplan-Meier
gold_fields.limitations: Retrospective, single-center, IHC scoring subjective

gold_fields.objective: To evaluate TMB as a biomarker for immunotherapy response in hepatocellular carcinoma.
gold_fields.method: Retrospective analysis of 452 HCC patients receiving PD-1 inhibitors, TMB measured by NGS.
gold_fields.key_findings: High TMB (≥10 mut/Mb) associated with better response to PD-1 inhibitors. OR=2.8, p=0.003.
gold_fields.conclusion: TMB is a predictive biomarker for PD-1 inhibitor response in HCC.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: ROC curve for TMB; Figure 2: TMB distribution by response status
gold_fields.limitations: Retrospective, TMB cutoff not validated in prospective studies, heterogeneity in NGS panels

gold_fields.objective: To evaluate FOLFOX regimen outcomes in stage III colorectal cancer.
gold_fields.method: Retrospective cohort, 856 patients, FOLFOX adjuvant chemotherapy.
gold_fields.key_findings: FOLFOX regimen achieved 72% 5-year DFS with manageable toxicity.
gold_fields.conclusion: FOLFOX is an effective and safe adjuvant regimen for stage III colorectal cancer.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: DFS Kaplan-Meier curve; Figure 2: Toxicity bar chart
gold_fields.limitations: Retrospective, single-center, no central pathology review

gold_fields.objective: To evaluate ctDNA for recurrence monitoring in resected NSCLC.
gold_fields.method: Longitudinal ctDNA analysis in 250 resected NSCLC patients using personalized assays.
gold_fields.key_findings: Postoperative ctDNA detection predicted recurrence with 89% sensitivity and 4.2 months lead time.
gold_fields.conclusion: ctDNA is a powerful tool for early recurrence detection in resected NSCLC.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: Detection timing; Figure 2: Recurrence probability by ctDNA status
gold_fields.limitations: Single institution, limited to NSCLC, assay cost high

gold_fields.objective: To evaluate patient-derived organoids for drug screening in pancreatic cancer.
gold_fields.method: Organoid culture from 115 pancreatic cancer patients, drug sensitivity testing.
gold_fields.key_findings: Organoids accurately predicted chemotherapy response with 85% sensitivity.
gold_fields.conclusion: Patient-derived organoids are a promising platform for personalized drug screening.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: Organoid brightfield images; Figure 2: Drug sensitivity heatmap
gold_fields.limitations: Low establishment rate, time-consuming, limited to chemotherapy agents

gold_fields.objective: To investigate CD8+ T cell infiltration as a biomarker for PD-1 blockade response in NSCLC.
gold_fields.method: Immunohistochemistry of tumor biopsies from 500 NSCLC patients, correlation with anti-PD-1 response.
gold_fields.key_findings: High CD8+ T cell infiltration associated with better response to PD-1 inhibitors. OR=3.5, 95% CI 2.1-5.8, p<0.001.
gold_fields.conclusion: CD8+ T cell infiltration is a predictive biomarker for PD-1 inhibitor response in NSCLC.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: IHC representative images; Figure 2: Response by CD8 density
gold_fields.limitations: Tissue availability, retrospective, threshold not prospectively validated

gold_fields.objective: To evaluate PIK3CA mutations and endocrine therapy resistance in ER-positive breast cancer.
gold_fields.method: Retrospective analysis of 489 ER-positive breast cancer patients receiving endocrine therapy, PIK3CA genotyping.
gold_fields.key_findings: PIK3CA mutations associated with resistance to endocrine therapy. median PFS 14.8 vs 24.2 months, p<0.01.
gold_fields.conclusion: PIK3CA mutations confer resistance to endocrine therapy in ER-positive breast cancer.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: PFS Kaplan-Meier; Figure 2: Mutation heatmap
gold_fields.limitations: Retrospective, single-center, heterogeneous endocrine therapy regimens

gold_fields.objective: To evaluate pembrolizumab plus chemotherapy versus chemotherapy alone in PD-L1 positive metastatic triple-negative breast cancer.
gold_fields.method: Phase III RCT, 847 patients with metastatic TNBC, pembrolizumab + chemotherapy vs chemotherapy.
gold_fields.key_findings: Pembrolizumab plus chemotherapy improved PFS in PD-L1 positive metastatic TNBC. HR=0.65, 95% CI 0.49-0.86, p=0.001.
gold_fields.conclusion: Pembrolizumab plus chemotherapy is a new standard of care for PD-L1 positive metastatic TNBC.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: PFS Kaplan-Meier; Figure 2: OS interim analysis
gold_fields.limitations: Limited to PD-L1 CPS≥10 patients; OS data immature

gold_fields.objective: To evaluate atezolizumab plus bevacizumab versus sorafenib in unresectable hepatocellular carcinoma.
gold_fields.method: Phase III RCT, 501 patients with unresectable HCC, atezolizumab + bevacizumab vs sorafenib.
gold_fields.key_findings: Atezolizumab plus bevacizumab significantly improved OS and PFS compared to sorafenib. OS HR=0.58, p<0.001; PFS HR=0.59, p<0.001.
gold_fields.conclusion: Atezolizumab plus bevacizumab is a new standard of care for unresectable hepatocellular carcinoma.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: OS Kaplan-Meier; Figure 2: PFS Kaplan-Meier
gold_fields.limitations: Limited to patients without prior systemic therapy; Asian patients overrepresented

gold_fields.objective: To evaluate talazoparib versus chemotherapy in BRCA-mutated advanced breast cancer.
gold_fields.method: Phase III RCT, 431 patients with BRCA-mutated advanced breast cancer, talazoparib vs chemotherapy.
gold_fields.key_findings: Talazoparib improved PFS compared to chemotherapy in BRCA-mutated advanced breast cancer. HR=0.54, 95% CI 0.41-0.71, p<0.001.
gold_fields.conclusion: Talazoparib is an effective treatment for BRCA-mutated advanced breast cancer.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: PFS Kaplan-Meier; Figure 2: Subgroup analysis
gold_fields.limitations: Limited to BRCA-mutated patients; no OS benefit demonstrated

gold_fields.objective: To evaluate olaparib maintenance therapy in BRCA-mutated pancreatic cancer.
gold_fields.method: Phase III RCT, 154 patients with BRCA-mutated pancreatic cancer, olaparib vs placebo maintenance.
gold_fields.key_findings: Olaparib maintenance improved PFS in BRCA-mutated pancreatic cancer. HR=0.53, 95% CI 0.35-0.82, p=0.004.
gold_fields.conclusion: Olaparib maintenance therapy is effective in BRCA-mutated pancreatic cancer.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: PFS Kaplan-Meier; Figure 2: Subgroup analysis
gold_fields.limitations: Small sample size; limited to BRCA-mutated patients; no OS benefit shown

gold_fields.objective: To evaluate nivolumab plus chemotherapy versus chemotherapy alone in advanced esophageal squamous cell carcinoma.
gold_fields.method: Phase III RCT, 1,323 patients, nivolumab + chemotherapy vs chemotherapy.
gold_fields.key_findings: Nivolumab plus chemotherapy significantly improved OS compared to chemotherapy alone. HR=0.74, 95% CI 0.62-0.88, p<0.001.
gold_fields.conclusion: Nivolumab plus chemotherapy is a new standard of care for advanced esophageal squamous cell carcinoma.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: OS Kaplan-Meier; Figure 2: PFS Kaplan-Meier
gold_fields.limitations: Open-label design; limited to Asian population in this analysis

gold_fields.objective: To evaluate sotorasib in KRAS G12C-mutated advanced solid tumors.
gold_fields.method: Phase I/II trial, 129 patients with KRAS G12C mutations, sotorasib 960mg QD.
gold_fields.key_findings: Sotorasib showed durable clinical benefit with ORR of 32% in KRAS G12C-mutated tumors.
gold_fields.conclusion: Sotorasib is the first effective targeted therapy for KRAS G12C-mutated solid tumors.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: Waterfall plot; Figure 2: PFS Kaplan-Meier
gold_fields.limitations: Small sample size; early phase trial; limited to KRAS G12C mutation

gold_fields.objective: To evaluate long-term outcomes of CTLA-4 and PD-1 blockade in advanced melanoma.
gold_fields.method: Phase III RCT, 945 patients, combination vs PD-1 alone vs CTLA-4 alone.
gold_fields.key_findings: Combination therapy showed 52% 5-year survival vs 34% for PD-1 alone and 26% for CTLA-4 alone.
gold_fields.conclusion: Combined CTLA-4 and PD-1 blockade provides the best long-term survival in advanced melanoma.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: OS Kaplan-Meier; Figure 2: Landmark analysis
gold_fields.limitations: High toxicity with combination therapy; biomarker data lacking

gold_fields.objective: To evaluate MEK inhibitors in KRAS-mutant colorectal cancer.
gold_fields.method: Phase II single-arm trial, 112 KRAS-mutant colorectal cancer patients.
gold_fields.key_findings: MEK inhibitors showed 34% disease control rate with PFS of 3.6 months.
gold_fields.conclusion: MEK inhibitors have modest activity in KRAS-mutant colorectal cancer, combination strategies needed.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: Waterfall plot of tumor change; Figure 2: PFS Kaplan-Meier
gold_fields.limitations: Single-arm; modest sample size; no biomarker for response identified

gold_fields.objective: To compare trastuzumab emtansine vs lapatinib plus capecitabine in HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer.
gold_fields.method: Phase III RCT, 600 patients, T-DM1 vs lapatinib + capecitabine.
gold_fields.key_findings: T-DM1 improved OS compared to lapatinib plus capecitabine in HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer. HR=0.68, 95% CI 0.54-0.86, p=0.01.
gold_fields.conclusion: T-DM1 is superior to lapatinib plus capecitabine in HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: OS Kaplan-Meier; Figure 2: PFS Kaplan-Meier
gold_fields.limitations: Limited to second-line therapy; high cost; limited to HER2-positive

gold_fields.objective: To review immunotherapy resistance mechanisms in triple-negative breast cancer.
gold_fields.method: Comprehensive literature review of immunotherapy resistance in TNBC.
gold_fields.key_findings: Resistance mechanisms include PD-L1 downregulation, loss of antigen presentation, suppressive TME.
gold_fields.conclusion: Overcoming immunotherapy resistance in TNBC requires combination strategies targeting multiple resistance mechanisms.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: Mechanisms of resistance schematic
gold_fields.limitations: Review article; no primary data; hypotheses not clinically validated

gold_fields.objective: To report 5-year survival outcomes of durvalumab consolidation therapy after chemoradiotherapy in stage III NSCLC.
gold_fields.method: Phase III RCT, 713 patients with unresectable stage III NSCLC, durvalumab 10mg/kg Q2W vs placebo for 12 months after chemoradiotherapy.
gold_fields.result_value: OS HR=0.72; PFS HR=0.55; 5-year OS=42.9% vs 33.4%
gold_fields.conclusion: Durvalumab after chemoradiotherapy provides robust and sustained OS and PFS benefit in unresectable stage III NSCLC.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: OS Kaplan-Meier; Figure 2: PFS Kaplan-Meier; Figure 3: Landmark analysis
gold_fields.limitations: Exploratory analysis; patients with EGFR/ALK mutations excluded; limited to patients without progression after chemoradiotherapy

gold_fields.objective: To evaluate anti-CD30 CAR-T therapy combined with PD-1 inhibitor in relapsed/refractory CD30+ lymphoma.
gold_fields.method: Phase II multicenter trial, 12 patients with r/r CD30+ lymphoma, anti-CD30 CAR-T ± anti-PD-1 antibody.
gold_fields.key_findings: ORR 91.7% (11/12), CR 50% (6/12); combination cohort achieved 100% ORR and 80% CR.
gold_fields.result_value: ORR=91.7%, CR=50%, median follow-up=21.5 months, PFS=45%, OS=70%
gold_fields.conclusion: Anti-PD-1 antibody enhances CD30 CAR-T therapy efficacy in r/r CD30+ lymphoma with minimal toxicity.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: Consort diagram; Figure 2: Response waterfall plot; Figure 3: Cytokine dynamics
gold_fields.limitations: Small sample size; single-arm; limited to CD30+ lymphomas

gold_fields.objective: To evaluate the prognostic utility of the Lung Immune Prognostic Index (LIPI) in urological malignancies treated with ICIs.
gold_fields.method: Meta-analysis of 7 retrospective cohort studies, 2,498 patients with urological malignancies receiving ICIs.
gold_fields.key_findings: Good LIPI associated with significantly better OS (good vs poor: HR=0.15, p<0.001) and PFS (HR=0.23, p<0.001).
gold_fields.result_value: OS HR=0.15 (good vs poor), PFS HR=0.23 (good vs poor), p<0.001
gold_fields.conclusion: LIPI is a valuable prognostic biomarker in urological malignancies treated with ICIs.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: PRISMA flow diagram; Figure 2: OS forest plot; Figure 3: PFS forest plot
gold_fields.limitations: Limited to retrospective studies; heterogeneity across studies; limited to urological malignancies

gold_fields.objective: To review resistance mechanisms to EGFR inhibitors in NSCLC and emerging therapeutic strategies.
gold_fields.method: Comprehensive review of EGFR-TKI resistance mechanisms and emerging therapies.
gold_fields.key_findings: Resistance mechanisms include T790M (~50% after 1st/2nd-gen TKIs), C797S, MET/HER2 amplification, and histologic transformation.
gold_fields.conclusion: Understanding resistance mechanisms is critical for optimizing patient outcomes and guiding personalized therapeutic approaches.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: Resistance mechanisms schematic; Figure 2: Emerging therapeutic strategies
gold_fields.limitations: Review article; no primary data; emerging therapies not clinically validated

gold_fields.objective: To investigate whether delta-radiomics features from contrast-enhanced CT predict survival in mCRC liver metastases treated with cetuximab rechallenge plus avelumab.
gold_fields.method: Retrospective substudy of CAVE phase II trial, 42 patients with mCRC liver metastases, radiomic feature analysis.
gold_fields.key_findings: Decrease in homogeneity independently correlated with longer PFS (HR=0.32, p=0.003) and OS (HR=0.41, p=0.021).
gold_fields.result_value: PFS HR=0.32, p=0.003; OS HR=0.41, p=0.021; AUC=0.94 training, 0.74 validation
gold_fields.conclusion: Delta-radiomics may serve as a reproducible imaging biomarker of response and survival in mCRC patients receiving cetuximab plus avelumab.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: ROC curves; Figure 2: Decision curve analysis; Figure 3: PFS/OS Kaplan-Meier
gold_fields.limitations: Small sample size; exploratory study; requires prospective validation; potential for overfitting

gold_fields.objective: To evaluate biomarker-driven non-standard targeted treatment outcomes in advanced cancer patients in routine care.
gold_fields.method: Multicenter non-interventional cohort study, 499 patients with advanced solid/hematological malignancies, biomarker-informed NSTT.
gold_fields.biomarker: PD-(L)1 expression (30.7%), HER2 aberrations (9.8%), BRAF mutations (9.0%)
gold_fields.key_findings: ORR 27.3%, 31.7% of patients had PFS ratio ≥1.3; checkpoint inhibitors were most common treatment.
gold_fields.conclusion: Biomarker-informed NSTT provides clinical benefit in 31.7% of patients, supporting precision oncology implementation in routine practice.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: Study flow diagram; Figure 2: PFS distribution; Figure 3: Biomarker breakdown
gold_fields.limitations: Non-interventional design; heterogeneous patient population; no control group

gold_fields.objective: To correlate CAR-T infusion product characteristics with clinical response using single-cell RNA sequencing.
gold_fields.method: Phase II trial, 24 patients with B-cell lymphoma/leukemia, third-generation CD19 CAR-T, targeted scRNA-seq.
gold_fields.key_findings: Effector-like CD8+ CAR-Ts with high polyfunctionality and low dysfunctional signature were associated with clinical response.
gold_fields.conclusion: Cell-intrinsic characteristics of effector CD8+ CAR-Ts correlate with response and can guide CAR-T manufacturing practices.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: Single-cell transcriptomic landscape; Figure 2: Response-associated CAR-T signatures; Figure 3: Manufacturing impact analysis
gold_fields.limitations: Small sample size; correlative study; limited to CD19 CAR-T

gold_fields.objective: To evaluate long-term efficacy and safety of sotorasib in KRAS G12C-mutated advanced NSCLC.
gold_fields.method: Phase II single-arm trial, 126 patients with KRAS G12C-mutated advanced NSCLC, sotorasib 960mg QD.
gold_fields.key_findings: ORR 37.1%, median PFS 6.8 months, median OS 12.5 months; 2-year OS rate 30.9%.
gold_fields.result_value: ORR=37.1%, median PFS=6.8 months, median OS=12.5 months
gold_fields.conclusion: Sotorasib demonstrates durable clinical benefit in heavily pretreated KRAS G12C-mutated NSCLC.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: Waterfall plot; Figure 2: PFS Kaplan-Meier; Figure 3: OS Kaplan-Meier
gold_fields.limitations: Single-arm; small sample size; limited to KRAS G12C mutation

gold_fields.objective: To report 5-year outcomes of nivolumab plus ipilimumab in advanced NSCLC.
gold_fields.method: Phase III RCT, patients with advanced NSCLC, nivolumab+ipilimumab vs chemotherapy.
gold_fields.key_findings: 5-year OS rate 24% for nivolumab+ipilimumab vs 14% for chemotherapy in PD-L1≥1% patients.
gold_fields.conclusion: Nivolumab plus ipilimumab provides durable long-term survival benefit in advanced NSCLC.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: OS Kaplan-Meier by PD-L1; Figure 2: Subgroup analysis; Figure 3: Landmark analysis
gold_fields.limitations: Limited to PD-L1 subgroups; high toxicity with combination

gold_fields.objective: To evaluate ceritinib versus chemotherapy in ALK-positive advanced NSCLC.
gold_fields.method: Phase III RCT, 376 patients with ALK-positive advanced NSCLC, ceritinib vs platinum-based chemotherapy.
gold_fields.key_findings: Ceritinib significantly improved PFS vs chemotherapy (16.6 vs 8.1 months, HR=0.55, p<0.001).
gold_fields.conclusion: Ceritinib is an effective first-line therapy for ALK-positive advanced NSCLC.
gold_fields.figures_summary: Figure 1: PFS Kaplan-Meier; Figure 2: OS interim analysis; Figure 3: Subgroup analysis
gold_fields.limitations: Limited to ALK-positive patients; OS data immature at analysis

gold_fields.objective: To determine the efficacy of an intensive multidisciplinary rehabilitation program in improving biomolecular and functional parameters in Parkinson's disease.
gold_fields.method: Phase III RCT protocol, 72 participants randomized to experimental (n=36) or home-based control (n=36), 6-week rehabilitation program.
gold_fields.biomarker: Oligomeric alpha-synuclein and neurotrophic factors in peripheral neural derived extracellular vesicles
gold_fields.key_findings: Study will evaluate effects on molecular biomarkers (oligomeric alpha-synuclein and neurotrophic factors) and secondary outcomes including motor/non-motor symptoms.
gold_fields.conclusion: This rehabilitation program is expected to provide evidence on impact of aerobic/intensive rehabilitation on brain plasticity in Parkinson's disease.
gold_fields.limitations: Study protocol; results pending; small sample size

gold_fields.objective: To assess the clinical relevance of lncRNA ST8SIA6-AS1 across malignancies and elucidate its functions in prostate cancer.
gold_fields.method: Meta-analysis of 11 studies (2,392 patients) plus in vitro/in vivo experiments (prostate cancer cell lines and xenograft).
gold_fields.model_system: Prostate cancer cell lines, xenograft tumor model
gold_fields.key_findings: High ST8SIA6-AS1 expression correlated with reduced OS (HR=1.48, 95% CI:1.31-1.65), increased tumor size (OR=2.07), and advanced TNM stage (OR=2.83).
gold_fields.result_value: OS HR=1.48, p<0.001; tumor size OR=2.07; TNM stage OR=2.83
gold_fields.conclusion: ST8SIA6-AS1 is a significant oncogenic lncRNA and prognostic marker for adverse outcomes in cancer.
gold_fields.limitations: Limited to retrospective studies; heterogeneity across cancer types

gold_fields.objective: To evaluate safety and efficacy of humanized CD19 CAR-T cells (hCART19) in relapsed/refractory B-cell non-Hodgkin lymphoma.
gold_fields.method: Clinical trial, 26 patients with R/R B-NHL, hCART19 infusion.
gold_fields.key_findings: ORR 80.8% (CR 69.2%, PR 11.5%); 1-year OS 65.8%, PFS 54.8%; no ICANS observed.
gold_fields.conclusion: Humanized CD19 CAR-T demonstrates favorable efficacy and manageable toxicity in R/R B-NHL.
gold_fields.limitations: Single-arm; small sample size; single-center

gold_fields.objective: To review biomarkers for predicting immune-related adverse events (irAEs) from immune checkpoint inhibitors.
gold_fields.method: Comprehensive review of mechanisms of ICIs, irAEs, and predictive biomarkers.
gold_fields.key_findings: ICIs are associated with life-threatening irAEs; biomarkers including cytokines, chemokines, autoantibodies, and microbiome composition may predict irAE development.
gold_fields.conclusion: Biomarkers for predicting irAEs are emerging but require further validation for clinical implementation.
gold_fields.limitations: Review article; no primary data

gold_fields.objective: To develop and validate an AI-based computational histology biomarker for risk stratification in metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer.
gold_fields.method: Phase III RCT data from CHAARTED (development, n=507) and ENZAMET (validation, n=684), AI-powered digital pathology.
gold_fields.key_findings: Unfavorable-risk group had worse OS (HR=2.6, 95% CI:2.0-3.4) and PFS (HR=2.2, 95% CI:1.7-2.7); 5-year OS 39% vs 69%.
gold_fields.conclusion: Image-only AI biomarker predicts clinical outcomes and potential benefit from treatment escalation in mHSPC.
gold_fields.limitations: Retrospective analysis of trial data; requires prospective validation

gold_fields.objective: To examine the prognostic value of fibrinogen-to-albumin ratio (FAR) in colorectal cancer.
gold_fields.method: Meta-analysis of 10 studies, 4,704 patients, pooled HRs for OS and PFS.
gold_fields.key_findings: Elevated FAR associated with worse OS (HR=1.59, 95% CI:1.38-1.83) and PFS (HR=1.65, 95% CI:1.44-1.90).
gold_fields.conclusion: Elevated FAR is strongly associated with poor OS and PFS in colorectal cancer, suggesting its potential as a prognostic marker.
gold_fields.limitations: Heterogeneity across studies; retrospective design

gold_fields.objective: To investigate safety and feasibility of axicabtagene ciloleucel (axi-cel) CAR-T administered in outpatient setting for R/R large B-cell lymphoma.
gold_fields.method: Phase II open-label multicenter study, 30 patients, outpatient axi-cel with prophylactic steroids.
gold_fields.key_findings: ORR 93% (CR 76%); grade ≥3 CRS 0%; grade ≥3 neurologic events 23%; 93% hospitalized with median stay 8 days.
gold_fields.conclusion: Outpatient administration of axi-cel is feasible with manageable safety profile in R/R LBCL.
gold_fields.limitations: Single-arm; small sample size; highly selected outpatient population

gold_fields.objective: To review the role of exosomes and circulating exosomal biomarkers in predicting response to immune checkpoint inhibitors.
gold_fields.method: Comprehensive review of exosomal biomarkers in cancer immunotherapy.
gold_fields.key_findings: Exosomal biomarkers can reflect dynamic changes in tumor and immune system and may serve as reliable indicators of immunotherapy response.
gold_fields.conclusion: Exosomal biomarkers show promise for predicting ICI response but require standardization and validation for clinical practice.
gold_fields.limitations: Review article; no primary data

gold_fields.objective: To consolidate evidence on diagnostic, prognostic, and therapeutic value of ctDNA in non-viral head and neck squamous cell carcinoma.
gold_fields.method: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 47 studies, 3,574 patients, pooled HRs for OS/PFS/RFS.
gold_fields.key_findings: ctDNA detection associated with worse OS (HR=10.26, 95% CI:3.58-29.40); residual ctDNA correlated with worse PFS (HR=7.32) and RFS (HR=7.33).
gold_fields.conclusion: ctDNA holds potential for improving diagnostic accuracy, monitoring progression, and predicting survival in non-viral HNSCC.
gold_fields.limitations: Heterogeneous methodologies; small studies; requires standardization

gold_fields.objective: To evaluate long-term outcomes and curative potential of CAR-T cell therapy in B-cell non-Hodgkin lymphomas.
gold_fields.method: Long-term follow-up of 38 patients treated with CTL019 (tisagenlecleucel), median follow-up 10.1 years.
gold_fields.sample_size: 38 patients (24 large B-cell lymphoma, 14 follicular lymphoma)
gold_fields.key_findings: 10-year lymphoma-free survival: 32% in large B-cell lymphoma, 47% in follicular lymphoma; no relapses beyond 5.4 years.
gold_fields.conclusion: A single CAR-T infusion led to decade-long remissions in approximately one third of large B-cell lymphoma and nearly half of follicular lymphoma patients.
gold_fields.limitations: Small sample size; single-arm; early CAR-T construct

gold_fields.objective: To evaluate the association between baseline circulating tumor DNA (ctDNA) gene alterations and survival outcomes in patients with RAS wild-type metastatic colorectal cancer treated with panitumumab plus chemotherapy.
gold_fields.method: Prespecified exploratory biomarker analysis of the phase 3 PARADIGM trial (n=802), focusing on ctDNA alterations in KRAS, NRAS, PTEN, EGFR, HER2, MET, ALK, RET, and NTRK1.
gold_fields.biomarker: ctDNA gene alterations (KRAS, NRAS, PTEN, EGFR, HER2, MET, ALK, RET, NTRK1)
gold_fields.key_findings: Overall survival was prolonged with panitumumab plus FOLFOX6 vs bevacizumab plus FOLFOX6 in patients with ctDNA lacking alterations (HR 0.76, 95% CI 0.62-0.92), but not in patients with ctDNA alterations.
gold_fields.result_value: OS HR=0.76 (negative hyperselected) vs HR=1.13 (ctDNA alterations)
gold_fields.conclusion: Negative hyperselection using ctDNA may guide optimal treatment selection in patients with metastatic colorectal cancer [citation:3].
gold_fields.limitations: Exploratory analysis, limited to RAS wild-type patients, requires prospective validation

gold_fields.objective: To review the mechanistic synergy and clinical evidence for integrating autologous stem cell transplantation with CAR-T therapy in B-cell lymphoma.
gold_fields.method: Comprehensive review of ASCT and CAR-T combination strategies in B-cell lymphoma.
gold_fields.key_findings: Integration of ASCT with CAR-T may leverage ASCT-induced immune reconstitution to enhance CAR-T persistence and reprogram the tumor microenvironment, with early clinical evidence suggesting improved disease control and progression-free survival.
gold_fields.conclusion: The combination of ASCT and CAR-T therapy represents a synergistic strategy for B-cell lymphoma, though challenges remain in toxicity, sequencing, and CAR-T longevity [citation:6].
gold_fields.limitations: Review article; no primary data; preliminary clinical evidence

gold_fields.objective: To explore the role of single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs) in cancer immunotherapy, focusing on genes involved in immune checkpoint regulation.
gold_fields.method: Systematic review of 29 studies across PubMed and Cochrane databases (2000-2024), assessing SNPs in CTLA-4, PD-1, and PD-L1 genes.
gold_fields.biomarker: SNPs in CTLA-4, PD-1, PD-L1 genes
gold_fields.key_findings: Specific SNPs in CTLA-4, PD-1, and PD-L1 genes influence both treatment outcomes and risk of immune-related adverse events; CTLA-4 SNPs such as -1722T>C and CT60G>A correlate with toxicity risk and survival.
gold_fields.conclusion: SNPs in immune checkpoint genes show promise as predictive biomarkers for immunotherapy, but further validation is needed for clinical implementation [citation:4].
gold_fields.limitations: Review article; no primary data; heterogeneity across studies

gold_fields.objective: To consolidate data from systematic reviews and meta-analyses on the efficacy and safety of CAR-T therapy in hematologic malignancies.
gold_fields.method: Umbrella review of 105 meta-analyses, systematic search of PubMed, Embase, Web of Science, and Cochrane up to May 2024.
gold_fields.key_findings: CD19-targeted CAR-T therapies demonstrated superior efficacy in ALL and DLBCL; combination with HSCT improved CR rates but increased severe adverse events; axi-cel carries higher risk of ICANS and neutropenia compared to tisa-cel.
gold_fields.conclusion: CAR-T therapy shows promising outcomes in ALL and DLBCL, but significant safety concerns remain; future research should optimize constructs and identify predictive biomarkers [citation:9].
gold_fields.limitations: Umbrella review; heterogeneity across primary studies

gold_fields.objective: To evaluate the impact of monocyte-related markers on immune checkpoint inhibitor efficacy and immune-related adverse events in cancer patients.
gold_fields.method: Systematic review and Bayesian meta-analysis of 155 eligible studies, searching PubMed, Web of Science, and Embase (2000-2023).
gold_fields.biomarker: Monocyte/lymphocyte ratio (MLR), absolute monocyte count (AMC), monocytic myeloid-derived suppressor cells (m-MDSCs)
gold_fields.key_findings: High MLR was a prognostic biomarker for short PFS (HR=1.5, 95% CI 1.21-1.88) and OS (HR=1.52, 95% CI 1.13-2.08); increased classical monocytes were unfavorable predictors of survival.
gold_fields.conclusion: Baseline monocyte phenotyping may serve as a composite biomarker of response to ICI; monocyte-related variables may aid in risk assessment and treatment decision strategies [citation:11].
gold_fields.limitations: Heterogeneity across studies; more data needed on irAEs
